text:
Market today: Chip and tech names are trying to bounce after a global AI sell-off hammered the sector. The real driver is macro fear — two hidden inflation triggers (beyond oil) are raising the odds of a Fed rate hike, which crushes growth/tech multiples. On margin, that double-pressure hits you harder than most.

Your holdings: PLTR (-12.77%) is flashing SELL — down hard with no cushion and your stop at $85 is far below current price, meaning a lot more pain is possible before it triggers. AVGO (-12.45%) is your largest position and flagged TRIM — that's your biggest dollar risk right now. SPAL (-14.48%) is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; it's already deep in the red and margin amplifies that decay aggressively. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO territory — treat it as fragile until it builds a track record.

Watch: 1) Fed rate-hike language — any hardening crushes your entire book. 2) Chip sector follow-through on the Micron bounce — if SMH stalls, trim winners before losers get worse.

Bottom line: Protect capital on AVGO and PLTR first — margin turns manageable losses into account-threatening ones fast.
